semanticCore: Treacherous deception, perfidy, breaking covenants secretly, deceit, or severe betrayal

keyDerivations.meaning: Treacherous deceiver, highly perfidious, covenant-breaker, or betrayer

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَمَا يَجْحَدُ بِآيَاتِنَا إِلَّا كُلُّ خَتَّارٍ كَفُورٍ (31:32): And none rejects Our signs except every treacherous, perfidious deceiver and ingrate.
discardedNoise: Khatara (to cross the mind) - Kh-T-R (with T'a).
summaryEn: The root represents worst-case treachery or breaking of promises (Khatr), condemning those who call upon God in distress but betray their covenants once they are safely returned to land.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر الغدر الشديد الفظيع ونكث العهد في الخفاء والخديعة القبيحة (ختر)، واصفاً المعرض الغادر الناكث بـالـ (ختّار) الكفور.
rawText: تختّر 5 He (a man, TA) was, or became, languid, (تَفَتَّرَ,) and relaxed, and heavy, or sluggish, and fevered: (K:) he was, or became, languid in body, in consequence of disease &c.: (TA:) and his intellect became confused, from drinking milk and the like. (K.) And تختّرت نَفْسُهُ His soul [or stomach] was, or became, languid. (TA.) === He walked with the gait of him who is heavy, or sluggish. (K.) ختّرهُ 2 , inf. n. تَخْتِيرٌ, said of wine, or beverage, It corrupted, or disordered, his soul [or stomach], (Ibn-'Arafeh, K,) and rendered him relaxed. (Ibn-'Arafeh.) خَاتِرٌ (K) and [in an intensive sense] 🡻خَتَّارٌ (S, A, K) and 🡻خَتِيرٌ and 🡻خَتُورٌ and 🡻خِتِّيرٌ (K) One who acts, or behaves, with perfidy, treachery, or unfaithfulness: (S, K:) or with the foulest perfidy or treachery or unfaithfulness: (A, K:) or with deceit, guile, or circumvention: (K:) [or in a bad, or corrupt, manner: (see 1:) the second and following epithets signifying one who does so much, or frequently, or habitually.] خَتَرٌ Languor (خَدَرٌ), (K,) or the like thereof, (TA,) that betides on the occasion of drinking medicine or poison, (K, TA,) such as weakens and intoxicates. (TA.) خَتَرَتْ نَفْسُهُ 1 His soul [or stomach] became heavy; or heaved, or became agitated by a tendency to vomit; syn. خَبُثَتْ: (IAar, K:) and became in a corrupt, or disordered, state. (K.) ___ خَتَرَهُ, (S, K, *) aor. ﹻ and ﹹ, (K,) inf. n. خَتْرٌ (S, A, K) and خُتُورٌ, (K,) He acted, or behaved, towards him with perfidy, treachery, or unfaithfulness: (S, K: *) or with the foulest perfidy or treachery or unfaithfulness: (A, K:) or with deceit, guile, or circumvention: (K:) or in a bad, or corrupt, manner. (Ibn-'Arafeh.) And خَتَرَ بِالعَهْدِ [He was unfaithful, &c., to the compact, or covenant]. (TA, from a trad.) خَتُورٌ : see what follows. خَتِيرٌ : see what follows. خَتَّارٌ : see what follows. خِتِّيرٌ : see what follows.
